1.1: This is the word of Yahweh that came to Joel son of Pethuel.
1.2: Listen to this, you elders — and everyone living in the land, pay close attention. Has anything like this ever happened in your lifetime, or in your fathers' time?
1.3: Pass it on to your children. Let your children tell it to their children, and their children to the generation after them.
1.5:
Wake up, you drunkards, and weep!
Howl, all you who love your wine —
the new wine has been taken right off your lips.
1.7:
My vine has been laid waste, my fig tree ruined —
the bark stripped off, the branches broken and flung away,
the wood left bare and white.
1.8:
Mourn — like a young bride wearing sackcloth
and weeping for the husband she loved in her youth.
1.9:
The grain offering and the drink offering are gone —
cut off from the house of Yahweh.
The priests, Yahweh's ministers, mourn.
1.10:
The fields lie ruined; the very earth is in mourning.
The grain is gone, the new wine has failed,
the olive oil has dried to nothing.
1.11:
Farmers, hang your heads in shame!
Vinedressers, wail —
the wheat and barley harvests are gone,
everything the fields were to yield.
1.12:
The vine is dry, the fig tree shrivelled;
pomegranate, palm, and apple tree —
every tree of the field has withered.
And with them, all joy has withered
from the children of Adam.
1.13:
Dress yourselves in sackcloth and lament, O priests!
Howl, you ministers of the altar!
Come, spend the night in sackcloth, you who serve my God —
for the grain offering and drink offering
have been cut off from your God's house.
1.14:
Call a fast. Summon a solemn assembly.
Bring together the elders and every inhabitant of the land
to the house of Yahweh your God,
and cry out to Yahweh.
1.15:
What a day that will be —
for the Day of the LORD is near,
catastrophe surging from the Almighty himself.
(In Hebrew, the very word for destruction — shod — echoes the name Shaddai.)
1.16:
Can we not see it with our own eyes —
food gone, joy gone,
gladness stripped from the house of our God?
1.18:
Listen — the animals are groaning.
The cattle herds mill about bewildered: no pasture anywhere.
Even the flocks of sheep suffer.
1.19:
To you, Yahweh, I cry out —
fire has devoured the open pastures,
flame has burned every tree of the field.

2.1:
Sound the war-horn in Zion!
Raise the alarm on my holy mountain!
Let every inhabitant of the land tremble —
for the Day of the LORD is coming, and it is nearly here —
2.2:
a day of darkness and deep gloom,
a day of clouds and dense shadow —
like the dawn rolling over the mountains comes a great and powerful horde.
Nothing like it has ever been before,
and nothing like it will come again
through all the generations to come.
2.3:
Fire devours before them;
flame burns behind them.
Before their advance: a garden, like Eden.
Behind their passing: a wilderness of ruin.
Nothing, and no one, escapes.
2.4:
They look like horses;
like cavalry they charge forward.
2.5:
The sound of them is like war-chariots
clattering over mountain peaks,
like a wildfire crackling through dry stubble —
a powerful army in battle formation.
2.6:
Peoples writhe with terror at their approach;
every face drains of colour.
2.8:
They don't crowd each other;
each moves in his own assigned path.
Even the sharpest weapons cannot stop them —
they cut through without slowing down.
2.9:
They swarm through the city,
run along the walls,
climb into the houses,
slip in through the windows like a thief.
2.11:
Yahweh thunders at the head of his army —
his host is immense beyond reckoning;
the one who carries out his command is mighty.
The Day of the LORD is vast and terrifying —
who can possibly stand before it?
2.12:
'Even now,' Yahweh declares,
'return to me with all your heart —
with fasting, with weeping, with mourning.'
2.15:
Sound the horn in Zion —
call a fast, summon a solemn gathering.
2.16:
Bring everyone together — every generation, every class.
Assemble the elders; bring the children;
bring even the nursing infants.
The bridegroom must leave his room,
the bride her bridal canopy.
2.17:
Let the priests stand between the entrance porch and the altar, weeping,
and let them pray:
'Spare your people, Yahweh —
do not make your own heritage an object of scorn,
a joke for the nations to repeat.
Why should they be saying:
"Where is their God?"'
2.18:
Then Yahweh became fiercely jealous for his land
and took pity on his people.
2.19:
Yahweh answered his people and said:
'I am sending you grain and wine and oil —
you will be satisfied at last.
I will never again make you a disgrace
among the nations.'
2.20:
I will push the northern invader far away from you —
out into a dry and barren land,
his front ranks driven into the Dead Sea,
his rear into the Mediterranean.
The stench of his rotting corpses will rise up —
for he raised himself too high.
2.21:
Do not be afraid, O land —
be glad and rejoice,
for Yahweh has done something magnificent.
2.23:
Children of Zion, rejoice!
Be glad in Yahweh your God —
for he has sent the early rain because he is keeping faith with you.
He has poured down for you the autumn rains
and the spring rains, as it used to be.
2.24:
The threshing floors will be full of grain;
the wine vats and the oil vats will overflow.
2.25:
I will give back the years
that the swarming locust ate —
the hopping locust, the stripping locust, the cutting locust —
my great army that I unleashed against you.
2.26:
You will eat abundantly and be full,
and you will praise the name of Yahweh your God
who has done wonders among you.
My people will never be shamed again.
2.27:
And you will know —
that I am here, in the midst of Israel,
that I am Yahweh your God and there is no other.
My people will never be shamed again.
2.29:
Even on the servants — men and women alike —
I will pour out my Spirit in those days.
2.30:
I will set signs and wonders
in the sky and on the earth:
blood, and fire, and towering pillars of smoke.
2.31:
The sun will be turned to darkness
and the moon to blood —
before the great and terrible Day of the LORD arrives.
2.32:
And this is the promise:
every person who calls on the name of Yahweh will be delivered.
In Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be a company who escape —
just as Yahweh has said —
and among those survivors will be the ones Yahweh himself has called.

3.1:
For mark this well: in those days — at that appointed time —
when I reverse the exile of Judah and Jerusalem,
3.2:
I will assemble every nation
and bring them down to the Valley of Jehoshaphat —
the valley where Yahweh judges.
There I will put them on trial
on behalf of my people, my heritage Israel,
because they scattered them through the nations
and partitioned my land among themselves.
3.3:
They gambled over my people like plunder,
trading a boy for the price of a prostitute
and selling a girl for a drink of wine.
3.4:
And as for you — Tyre, Sidon,
and all the districts of Philistia —
what do you think you are doing to me?
Are you trying to settle a score?
If so, I will repay you
swiftly and directly — blow for blow.
3.5:
You took my silver and my gold;
my finest treasures you hauled off into your temples.
3.6:
You sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks —
shipping them as far from their homeland as possible.
3.7:
But I will rouse them from the very place you sold them to,
and I will turn everything you did back on your own heads.
3.8:
I will sell your own sons and daughters
into the hands of the people of Judah,
and they will trade them to the Sabeans — a nation far away.
Yahweh has spoken this.
3.9:
Announce this to every nation:
Prepare for war — mobilise!
Arouse all your fighting men;
let every soldier advance and come up!
3.10:
Beat your plowshares into swords,
your pruning hooks into spears.
Let even the weakest one boast: 'I am a soldier.'
(Joel here deliberately reverses the vision of Isaiah 2:4 and Micah 4:3:
not peace into war, but the opposite — summoning the nations to their doom.)
3.11:
Come quickly, all you surrounding nations —
gather yourselves there.
And you, Yahweh — bring down your warriors.
3.12:
Let the nations stir themselves and come up
to the Valley of Jehoshaphat —
for there I will take my seat
to judge every nation on every side.
3.13:
Swing the sickle — the harvest is ready.
Stamp down the winepress — it is full to the brim.
The vats are running over.
Their wickedness is that great.
3.14:
Multitudes — multitudes —
in the Valley of Decision, where the verdict will fall!
For the Day of the LORD is at the threshold,
right there in the Valley of Decision.
3.15:
Sun and moon go dark;
the stars pull back their light.
3.16:
Yahweh roars from Zion;
he thunders from Jerusalem.
Heaven and earth shake —
but Yahweh is a shelter for his people,
a fortress for the children of Israel.
3.17:
Then you will know —
I am Yahweh your God,
dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain.
Jerusalem will be set apart as holy,
and no foreign army will march through it again.
3.19:
But Egypt will become a wasteland
and Edom a barren desert —
because of what they did to Judah:
the violence, the innocent blood
they spilled in the land.
3.20:
Judah, however, will be inhabited forever;
Jerusalem, to every generation.
3.21:
I will avenge their blood —
every drop I have not yet called to account —
for Yahweh makes his home in Zion.
